title: Module: "What kind of study is this?" (design/phase/arms/blinding)
context: This is the first patient-facing educational module of the primer and the anchor for the M0 slice. It teaches cancer patient advocates to classify a study by design (RCT, observational, single-arm), phase (I–IV), arm structure, and blinding — foundational knowledge required to locate a study on the evidence hierarchy and apply any worksheet. It must be education, not advice, and is high risk-tier requiring dual sign-off.
objective: Author an original, plain-language educational module that teaches patient advocates to identify study type, design, trial phase, arm structure, and blinding, with every claim provenance-cited and dual high-tier sign-off obtained.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers: randomised controlled trial vs observational vs single-arm study; trial phases I, II, III, and IV and what each can and cannot show; arm structure (parallel, crossover, factorial); blinding (open-label, single-blind, double-blind, triple-blind) and its relevance to bias.
Written in plain language targeting a lay audience; no unexplained jargon without a glossary anchor.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice consistent with the policy (task 002).
Every factual and methodological claim is provenance-cited to an entry in data/sources.yaml with a compatible allowedUse; passes the licence + provenance build gate (task 004).
Content passes the advice-language checklist (task 002): no prescriptive second-person forms, no treatment recommendations, no individualised guidance.
CI passes: build, lint, licence + provenance gate, and readability check.
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as treatment guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): content is usable, accessible, and does not patronise or stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: study-design and phase descriptions are methodologically accurate.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Goals — Goal 1; § Solution approach — Content corpus)
output: A plain-language educational module (content/modules/what-kind-of-study.md or equivalent) committed to the repo, covering study design, phase, arms, and blinding — provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off.
